inline_review:
commit a9a088e69086b2dbd2b12133838cf1afe3826c80
Author: Youngjun Park <youngjun.park@lge.com>

PM: hibernate: fix spurious GFP mask WARNING in uswsusp path

This commit introduces pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe() to conditionally restore
the GFP mask only when saved_gfp_count is non-zero. It then updates the
uswsusp path to use this new helper to prevent a spurious warning introduced
by the new GFP mask stacking behavior.

Fixes: 35e4a69b2003f ("PM: sleep: Allow pm_restrict_gfp_mask() stacking")

[ ... ]

> diff --git a/include/linux/suspend.h b/include/linux/suspend.h
> --- a/include/linux/suspend.h
> +++ b/include/linux/suspend.h
> @@ -454,6 +454,7 @@ extern void pm_report_hw_sleep_time(u64 t);
>  extern void pm_report_max_hw_sleep(u64 t);
>  void pm_restrict_gfp_mask(void);
>  void pm_restore_gfp_mask(void);
> +void pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe(void);

Does this need an empty static inline fallback in the #else block for when
CONFIG_PM_SLEEP is disabled?

While current callers are protected by CONFIG_HIBERNATION, any future callers
using this API without CONFIG_PM_SLEEP might cause undefined reference
compilation failures.

[ ... ]

> diff --git a/kernel/power/main.c b/kernel/power/main.c
> --- a/kernel/power/main.c
> +++ b/kernel/power/main.c
> @@ -36,6 +36,24 @@
>  static unsigned int saved_gfp_count;
>  static gfp_t saved_gfp_mask;
>  
> +/**
> + * pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe - Conditionally restore the GFP mask
> + *
> + * Call pm_restore_gfp_mask() only if a GFP restriction is active.
> + *
> + * After GFP mask stacking was introduced, calling
> + * pm_restore_gfp_mask() without a matching restriction triggers a
> + * warning. Some hibernation paths invoke restore defensively, so this
> + * helper avoids spurious warnings when no restriction is in place.
> + */
> +void pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe(void)

Should dpm_resume_end() also be updated to use this new safe helper to fully
resolve the regression?

When the system transitions to a sleep state, dpm_suspend_start() calls
dpm_prepare(). If a device driver's prepare callback fails, it returns the
error immediately and skips calling pm_restrict_gfp_mask().

The caller handles this failure by initiating the cleanup path via
dpm_resume_end(), which currently calls pm_restore_gfp_mask()
unconditionally. Because pm_restrict_gfp_mask() was bypassed,
saved_gfp_count remains 0, which would trigger the same warning this
patch is fixing.
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Missing static inline fallback for pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe() when CONFIG_PM_SLEEP is disabled.","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"The new function pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe() is declared inside the #ifdef CONFIG_PM_SLEEP block in include/linux/suspend.h. However, unlike its sibling functions pm_restrict_gfp_mask() and pm_restore_gfp_mask(), it lacks an empty static inline fallback implementation in the corresponding #else block. While the current caller in kernel/power/user.c is compiled only when CONFIG_HIBERNATION is enabled, any future callers using this API without CONFIG_PM_SLEEP will cause compilation failures (undefined reference). This violates kernel API design contracts for conditionally compiled subsystems."},{"problem":"Incomplete fix: Missed spurious warning in device suspend error paths (dpm_resume_end).","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"When the system transitions to a sleep state, dpm_suspend_start() calls dpm_prepare(). If a device driver's prepare callback fails, dpm_suspend_start() returns the error immediately and skips calling pm_restrict_gfp_mask(). The caller handles this failure by initiating the cleanup path via dpm_resume_end(), which unconditionally calls pm_restore_gfp_mask(). Since pm_restrict_gfp_mask() was bypassed due to the dpm_prepare() failure, saved_gfp_count remains 0. This causes pm_restore_gfp_mask() to trigger the exact same WARN_ON(!saved_gfp_count) that this patch attempts to fix for the uswsusp path. To fully resolve the regression, dpm_resume_end() must also be updated to use the new pm_restore_gfp_mask_safe() helper."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit a9a088e69086b2dbd2b12133838cf1afe3826c80

> + */
